response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned senior engineer with strong geospatial experience (GISCollective, Green Map) and proven low-latency distributed-systems work at Sociomantic (DLang, millions of requests/second), which partially maps to the role. However, the core required stack — modern C++ or Rust — is barely represented: only 6 months of C/C++ at National Instruments in 2014-2015 and a university thesis. Recent work is predominantly TypeScript/Node.js. Worth a screening conversation given the geospatial pedigree and systems mindset, but C++ depth is a significant gap.
strengths: Deep geospatial/GIS background via GISCollective and Open Green Map
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (DLang at Sociomantic)
Senior engineer with strong testing/craftsmanship discipline (TDD, CI/CD)
concerns: Minimal and outdated C++ experience; no Rust
Recent roles centered on TypeScript/Node.js, not systems programming
No direct navigation/routing or map-tile pipeline experience
